raw_text: zayd aslam 91 wilmington gardens, barking, essex, ig119tr - 079 6474 8251 zaydaslam786@gmail.com aerospace engineering graduate, first class, with it infrastructure background that can bring expertise and experience from both fields to the table when tackling problems. education (2016 2020) beng aerospace engineering, university of hertfordshire first class (predicted) final year project: as hydrogen has high energy density compared to other storage methods, it is often advantageous for use in transport application. the paper focused on developing a methodology that can be used to design a system for any uav. by using a hydrogen fuel cell as the main power source, and an additional energy storage system to carry any transient loads, performance of the uav can be optimized. the methodology was then applied to a case study and simulated using matlab simscape where performance of the fuel cell was analysed. findings include the efficacy of the sizing methodology and limitations in hydrogen storage methods within aerospace applications. we are currently drafting a communication paper which will be published pending peer review. relevant modules: aero structural design and analysis, aerospace vehicle design, aerospace systems modelling and control, engineering application of mathematics, materials and electrical science, fluid mechanics and thermodynamics, aerodynamics. (2013 - 2014) it apprenticeship, firebrand btec level 3 diploma in professional competence for it and telecoms professionals btec level 3 certificate in ict systems and principles microsoft: qualifications include: network fundamentals, security fundamentals, + 3 additional 2008 - 2013 gsce & a-levels, barking abbey school / sixth form a-levels: biology, chemistry, physics, applied mathematics gcses: 11, including: english literature, english language, mathematics, and double science. experience july 2017 present (part-time) project engineer, am networking projects include: e set up secure documentation server on the aws cloud platform using docker and secured by allowing access to specific ips and only allowing encrypted connections. e implemented ubiquities cloud network ensuring it met their expectations. e provided support to customers, ensuring client service and customer satisfaction was paramount. e took charge of and managed projects from conception to delivery, often working in teams, and ensuring seniors and clients were up to date along the way. e managed, deployed, and supported network and cloud infrastructure. skills e system modelling using matlab, journal of power sources, where it will then be published, pending peer review. enjoy working on a variety of home projects, one of which was a solid-state cooler, based on thermoelectric cooling technology (rather than using phase change refrigeration). a second home project was building dml (distributed mode loudspeaker) speakers for my home computer, the aim was to build a system that gives a surround sound effect in a cost-effective manner. references available on request.
